heading: Use of domestic products
label: 3546 Use of domestic products
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992, and not as part of the Department of Housing and Urban Development Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Prohibition against fraudulent use of “Made in America” labels
children.label: a Prohibition against fraudulent use of “Made in America” labels
children.text: A person shall not intentionally affix a label bearing the inscription of “Made in America”, or any inscription with that meaning, to any product sold in or shipped to the United States, if that product is not a domestic product.

children.heading: Report
children.label: b Report
children.text: The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development and the Secretary of Agriculture shall each submit, before , a report to the Congress on procurements of products that are not domestic products.January 1, 1994
that is manufactured or produced in the United States; and
at least 50 percent of the cost of the articles, materials, or supplies of which are mined, produced, or manufactured in the United States.
